title: Welcome to the Space Cam!
description: NAU students are designing, building, and testing a dual-wavelength camera system that could support future interplanetary NASA missions. The VISIONS instrument is being developed to capture true-color imagery and surface temperature data at the same time, giving researchers a fuller picture of planetary conditions. The project also gives students hands-on experience meeting NASA's rigorous standards for flight-ready hardware.
imageAlt: People work on space technology hardware
impact: Radiant helps train the next generation of planetary scientists and engineers by giving students hands-on experience building real spaceflight hardware. Projects like VISIONS connect classroom learning with mission-ready instrumentation for future Mars and deep-space exploration.
